challenges.explanation: @dataclass generates __init__ (y defaults to 0) and a readable __repr__, so P(3) prints as P(x=3, y=0).
@dataclass generates __eq__ that compares by field values, so two P instances with the same x and y are equal → True. (A plain class would compare by identity and print False.)

challenges.prompt: Complete the line so instances are immutable.
challenges.explanation: @dataclass(frozen=True) makes instances immutable — assigning to a field afterward raises FrozenInstanceError (and instances become hashable).
